# BoundaryCI

> BoundaryCI is a local-first tenant-isolation security scanner and paid Cloud history service for SaaS teams using Supabase and PostgreSQL.

BoundaryCI detects authorization mistakes that can expose one SaaS customer's data to another customer. The open-source CLI reconstructs the final security state from ordered SQL migrations and applies deterministic checks for missing row-level security, unscoped authenticated policies, public access, and unsafe security-definer functions. An optional Fireworks AI review can identify subtler policy interactions.

The scanner runs locally or inside GitHub Actions without database credentials. BoundaryCI Cloud is opt-in and receives minimized, secret-redacted finding results rather than full migration files. Cloud provides repository-bound ingestion, scan history, finding evidence, subscription-aware usage, and team-wide visibility.

## Start here

- Public product page: https://boundaryci.com/
- Documentation and source: https://github.com/sir-gig/boundaryci
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/boundaryci
- GitHub Marketplace Action: https://github.com/marketplace/actions/boundaryci-tenant-isolation-scan
- Security policy: https://github.com/sir-gig/boundaryci/blob/main/SECURITY.md
- Privacy notice: https://github.com/sir-gig/boundaryci/blob/main/PRIVACY.md
- Terms: https://github.com/sir-gig/boundaryci/blob/main/EULA.md

## Verified product scope

- Six deterministic Supabase/PostgreSQL security rules
- Stable finding fingerprints, baselines, and expiring waivers
- Terminal, JSON, SARIF, and GitHub annotation output
- Optional schema-constrained Fireworks AI review
- Free plan: 100 Cloud scans per month
- Team: $49/month or $490/year for 1,000 Cloud scans per month
- Growth: $149/month or $1,490/year for 10,000 Cloud scans per month

BoundaryCI is a focused continuous security control. It does not claim to replace a penetration test, threat modeling, or an independent security assessment.
